channel.title: All Entries on Detention Pipeline
channel.description: Recent content in All Entries on Detention Pipeline

item.title: 10801 NW 97th St — Miami-Dade, FL (162000 SF)
item.description: 162K SF warehouse at Flagler Station in Miami-Dade, owned by AEW Capital Management, currently leased. Part of Miami Trading Zone industrial cluster.

item.title: 10Gfedsupply, LLC — ICE Mecklenburg, NC $704,997
item.description: Federal contract awarded to 10GFEDSUPPLY, LLC in NC ($704,996.57).

item.title: 11301 NW 97th Ave — Miami-Dade, FL (174400 SF)
item.description: 174K SF warehouse in Medley, Miami-Dade, owned by Hilco Redevelopment Partners, leased to Garland Food. Standard industrial property.

item.title: 12805 NW 42nd Ave — Miami-Dade, FL (235900 SF)
item.description: 236K SF Prologis distribution center in Opa-Locka, Miami-Dade, leased to Amazon. Active logistics property.

item.title: 13606 Furman Rd — Harris, TX (183000 SF)
item.description: 183K SF available warehouse in Houston, Harris County TX, divisible to 40K SF. Owned by Vault Distribution Center.

item.title: 1511 Zoo Pkwy — Duval, FL (250000 SF)
item.description: 250K SF Walmart distribution center in Jacksonville, Duval County FL, serving 18 Sam’s Clubs. Active retail logistics site.

item.title: 15810 Walgreens Dr — Palm Beach, FL (182800 SF)
item.description: 183K SF warehouse in Jupiter, Palm Beach FL, owned by Ambrose Property Group, leased to Niagara Bottling. Standard industrial property.

item.title: 54 Congressional Democrats Launch Investigation into ICE Warehouse Profiteering
item.description: 54 Congressional Democrats led by Raskin and Warren launched investigation into ICE warehouse profiteering on March 30, 2026. Documented 333-1000% markups and 33 admin members with Blue Owl investments.

item.title: Blue Owl Capital — Private Equity Firm Profiting from ICE Warehouse Purchases
item.description: Blue Owl Capital ($307.5B AUM) sold distressed warehouses to DHS at multiples of market value. Stock crashed 68% amid $5.4B in redemption requests; DHS sales were a financial lifeline.

item.title: Carlyle Group — Oakwood GA Warehouse Seller, 22 Admin Officials Hold Investments
item.description: Carlyle Group sold the Oakwood, GA warehouse to DHS for $68.2M. 22 Trump officials hold Carlyle investments, including former Carlyle employee Todd Newnam now at Treasury.

item.title: DHS Inspector General Launches Investigation into Noem/Lewandowski Contract Handling
item.description: DHS IG launched investigation into Noem/Lewandowski contract handling on March 26, 2026. Lewandowski allegedly solicited success fees from GEO Group; contract corruption was a catalyst for Noem’s firing.

item.title: DHS Secretary Mullin Pauses ICE Warehouse Purchases, Reviews All Noem-Era Contracts
item.description: DHS Secretary Mullin paused 11 ICE warehouse conversions and imposed $25M contract approval threshold on March 24, 2026, reviewing all Noem-era contracts after $364M in documented overpayments.

item.title: FOIA and Open Records Requests Needed — Warehouse Profiteering Investigation
item.description: Prioritized list of FOIA and state open records requests targeting NDAs with local officials, missing appraisals, WEXMAC-TITUS scope modifications, and Blue Owl ethics determinations.

item.title: Goldman Sachs — Double Exposure in ICE Warehouse Profiteering
item.description: Goldman Sachs has double ICE exposure: direct seller of Roxbury NJ ($129.3M, 137% markup) and lender on Williamsport MD ($352.7M refi). Combined Goldman-connected proceeds: $231.7M.

item.title: PNK Group — Russia-Founded Industrial Developer, Social Circle GA Seller, Sberbank Partner
item.description: Russia-founded industrial developer that sold Social Circle, GA warehouse to DHS for $128.6M after buying it for $29.4M. Founder Sharkov has Sberbank, Roscongress, and ICIJ offshore ties.

item.title: Project Salt Box — ICE Warehouse Overpayment Research and Tracking
item.description: Activist research org documenting ICE warehouse overpayments through property records and lien filings. Their data shows markups ranging from 137% to ~1000% across confirmed purchases.

item.title: Rockefeller Group (Mitsubishi Estate) — Surprise AZ Warehouse Seller
item.description: Mitsubishi Estate subsidiary that sold the Surprise, AZ warehouse to DHS for $70M vs ~$12M cost basis (483% markup). GardaWorld won the $313.4M conversion contract despite no detention experience.

item.title: WEXMAC-TITUS — How ICE Bypasses Normal Contracting Using Military Procurement
item.description: WEXMAC-TITUS is a Navy contracting vehicle repurposed for ICE detention, expanded from $10B to $65B ceiling. Bypasses competitive bidding, appraisals, and transparency required under normal GSA procurement.

item.title: BI Incorporated — ISAP V IDIQ Contract (Ankle Monitors & Surveillance)
item.description: $1B sole-source contract for BI Inc (GEO subsidiary) to run ICE’s ISAP V ankle monitor and SmartLINK surveillance program. ICE targets 180,000 on GPS monitors by 2026.

item.title: BI Incorporated — Skip Tracing Contract ($121M)
item.description: $121M contract for BI Inc to hunt immigrants who missed check-ins using its own ISAP surveillance data. Completes GEO Group’s monitor-hunt-detain closed loop.

item.title: Bradford County FL — Sabot Consulting IGSA Proposal ($239M/year at full capacity)
item.description: Sabot Consulting proposed a 3,000-bed phased IGSA detention facility in Bradford County FL worth $239M/year at full capacity. County commission voted 3-2 to advance.

item.title: Camp East Montana — $1.2B Army Contract for ICE Tent Detention Camp
item.description: $1.2B Army contract for a tent detention camp at Fort Bliss TX, awarded to a no-experience contractor at a Virginia residential address. Three deaths in first five months.

item.title: Clearview AI — $9.2M ICE facial recognition contract (Sept 2025)
item.description: $9.2M sole-source ICE contract with Clearview AI for facial recognition matching against a 50B+ image database. Justified for child exploitation and officer assault cases.

item.title: Coast Guard Aircraft Diverted to ICE Detainee Transport (2025)
item.description: At least 324 Coast Guard flights diverted to ICE detainee transport in 2025, using C-27J Spartans and C-130s as feeders for Air Force deportation flights from hub airports.

item.title: G4S Secure Solutions — ICE Ground Transportation & Custody Contracts
item.description: G4S (Allied Universal) holds multiple ICE ground transportation task orders across six field offices. A $100M San Diego contract was solicited for FY2026.

item.title: GEO Group — WEXMAC-TITUS Pre-Qualification (No-Bid Access to $45B)
item.description: GEO Group was quietly pre-qualified for WEXMAC-TITUS, giving it no-bid access to $45B in OBBBA detention funding. Announced on a Friday night in Feb 2026.

item.title: Glades County FL — Reinstated IGSA (up to 500 beds, April 2025)
item.description: ICE reinstated the Glades County FL IGSA on April 3, 2025 for up to 500 beds operated by the sheriff. Sabot Consulting featured it as a client success story.

item.title: ICE Air Operations — CSI Aviation Charter Flight Contract (FY2025-2026)
item.description: CSI Aviation’s ICE Air contract surged from $364M (FY2024) to $1.1B (FY2025). Domestic transfer flights jumped 132% as new remote detention facilities opened.

item.title: ICE Detention Medical Care Crisis (2025-2026)
item.description: 32 people died in ICE custody in 2025, the deadliest year in two decades. ICE stopped paying medical providers in Oct 2025, creating a ~$300M gap as detained population surged 82%.

item.title: ICE Skip Tracing Program — $1.2B across 13 contractors (Dec 2025)
item.description: $1.2B program across 13 private contractors to locate 1.5M immigrants for deportation using AI, databases, and physical surveillance. Operatives carry no DHS credentials.

item.title: LexisNexis LEIDS — $22.1M ICE + $15.9M CBP database subscription
item.description: $38M combined ICE ($22.1M) and CBP ($15.9M) contracts for LexisNexis LEIDS database access including Accurint dossiers and license plate recognition.

item.title: Military Infrastructure in the Detention System — Three Distinct Uses
item.description: Military installations serve three distinct detention functions: active-base detention (avoiding civilian oversight), former-base staging facilities, and ICE Air deportation flight hubs.

item.title: MVM Inc. — ICE Detainee Transportation Contract ($1.1B cumulative)
item.description: $1.1B cumulative MVM Inc. contracts for ICE detainee and unaccompanied child transportation. $145M in new 2025 contracts; owner family are major Trump donors.

item.title: Palantir ICM (Investigative Case Management) — $139.3M (2022-2026)
item.description: $139.3M Palantir contract for ICE’s Investigative Case Management system (2022-2026). Slated for sole-source renewal because no comparable system exists.

item.title: Palantir ImmigrationOS — $30M prototype + $29.9M maintenance (2025-2027)
item.description: $59.9M sole-source Palantir contracts to build ImmigrationOS, ICE’s deportation pipeline platform with AI targeting, confidence scoring, and self-deportation tracking.

item.title: The ICE Contract Pipeline — From Targeting to Deportation
item.description: Framework mapping the 7-stage ICE enforcement pipeline from targeting to deportation, each served by different contractors. GEO Group operates a closed loop across surveillance, skip-tracing, and detention.

item.title: Thomson Reuters CLEAR — $22.8M DHS/ICE database subscription (through 2026)
item.description: $22.8M five-year DHS contract for Thomson Reuters CLEAR database access. 2025 option confirms license plate reader data provision alongside utility and phone records.

item.title: Tracking Resources — Maps, Monitors, and Databases
item.description: Curated list of external resources for monitoring detention expansion: facility maps (Courier, Freedom for Immigrants), flight monitors (Human Rights First), investigative journalists, and FOIA tools.

item.title: Vigilant Solutions (Motorola) — $22.8M ICE ALPR database access (through 2026)
item.description: $22.8M ICE contract for Vigilant Solutions (Motorola) license plate reader database access through 2026. ICE also purchased mobile ALPR hardware for ERO field operations.
